scope: Press first-party source and development tooling

excludedRules.evidence: 1517 findings are dominated by dynamic service, options, browser callback, and test-harness signatures; enabling it would require a semantic cleanup campaign rather than a correctness gate.
862 findings primarily represent intentional best-effort browser cleanup and storage fallbacks whose failure must not replace the primary operation.
80 findings occur in regular-expression and fixture literals where mechanical edits create churn without strengthening runtime correctness.
10 findings are deliberate regular-expression literals; the repository contract tests cover the accepted inputs more directly.
3 findings are deliberate parser and sanitization character ranges covered by focused behavior tests.
59 findings arise in stateful parser, DOM, and compatibility flows where a repository-wide rewrite is not an incremental lint boundary.
4 findings are compatibility error translations; the public error contract is tested and does not expose Error.cause.

noGrowth.policy: All enabled rules have a zero-violation baseline; any new error or warning fails CI. Excluded rules are explicit accepted-no-action decisions whose exact diagnostic and affected-file counts are remeasured on every quality run.

eslint.removalCondition: Re-evaluate an excluded rule only when its evidence no longer applies; do not replace repository contract tests with generic lint unless the same invariant remains covered.

noGrowth.policy: New candidate files must pass Prettier. After bootstrap, an established path baseline may retain only untouched files or transfer through an exact-content rename; touching a legacy file requires formatting it and removing it from the baseline.

prettier.removalCondition: Remove a path after formatting that file intentionally; avoid release-bearing mass reformat commits.

evidence.command: tsc --allowJs --checkJs --noEmit --skipLibCheck --target ES2023 --module preserve --moduleResolution bundler --lib ES2023,DOM,DOM.Iterable with 227 non-vendor assets/js JS/MJS root inputs
evidence.reason: Repository-wide checkJs would create a second large suppression baseline across dynamic browser boundaries; even after separating transitive vendor output, first-party code retains 905 diagnostics in 83 files.

revisitWhen: Composer and editor modules have been grouped behind stable directory and service boundaries
Each extracted module can adopt checkJs or ts-check without a repository-wide compatibility baseline
